assertions.expectation: Strongly advises AGAINST moving to 'No channel' — it provides LESS control, not more
assertions.reason: Opens with strong statement 'No, moving to "No channel" would actually give you LESS control, not more' and calls it a common misconception

assertions.expectation: Explains that 'No channel' only supports the basic 30-day 'no upgrades' exclusion, while release channels support all three exclusion types including 'no minor or node upgrades' (up to EoS)
assertions.reason: Table clearly shows Regular channel supports 'no minor upgrades' and 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusions while No channel only has 'Only 30-day "no upgrades" exclusions'

assertions.expectation: Recommends staying on Regular channel (or moving to Stable/Extended) and using maintenance exclusions for control
assertions.reason: Explicitly states 'Stay on Regular channel' and provides gcloud commands for maintenance exclusions, also discusses Extended channel as an alternative

assertions.expectation: Specifically recommends the 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion as the way to prevent disruptive changes while allowing security patches
assertions.reason: Shows gcloud command for 'no_minor_or_node_upgrades' exclusion and explains it blocks minor/node upgrades while allowing control plane security patches

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended support (24 months) is only available on release channels, not 'No channel'
assertions.reason: Table shows Extended support available on Regular channel but not No channel, and Extended channel section mentions 'Up to 24 months of support per version'

assertions.expectation: Explains that 'No channel' is a legacy configuration and the industry is moving away from it
assertions.reason: Explicitly calls No channel 'a legacy configuration' in the explanation section

assertions.expectation: Mentions that maintenance windows control WHEN upgrades happen, while exclusions control WHAT upgrades happen — together they give fine-grained control
assertions.reason: While both maintenance windows and exclusions are discussed, the response doesn't explicitly articulate this distinction between WHEN vs WHAT controls

assertions.expectation: Warns about the risks of 'No channel': no granular exclusions, no Extended support, risk of snowflaking clusters
assertions.reason: Covers lack of granular exclusions and no Extended support, but doesn't mention the risk of snowflaking clusters or other operational risks of No channel
